name: Grilled Portobello Mushrooms with Parmesan Crrisps
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Teaspoons minced fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 teaspoon, crumbled dried
18 arugula leaves, trimmed, washed, and spun dry (18 to 24)
6 Parmesan crisps (recipe follows) as an accompaniment
3/4 cup finely shredded Parmesan cheese (preferably Parmigiano-Reggiano)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl whisk together vinegar, garlic, and thyme, and salt and pepper to taste. Whisk in oil in a stream, whisking until dressing is emulsified.
2. In a large resealable plastic bag drizzle dressing over mushrooms and seal. Marinate mushrooms at room temperature, turning the bag once or twice, 1 hour.
3. Heat a well-seasoned ridged grill pan over moderately high heat until hot. Grill mushrooms in batches, covered, 2 to 3 minutes on each side, or until tender. Transfer mushrooms as cooked to a platter and keep warm, covered with foil.
4. Divide arugula among 6 plates. Slice mushrooms thin and serve with arugula and Parmesan crisps.
PARMESAN CRISPS
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a lightly greased baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In a small bowl stir together Parmesan and flour. On the prepared baking sheet, spoon level tablespoons of mixture in mounds 4 inches apart and spread mounds gently into 3 1/2-inch-long ovals.
3. Bake crisps in the middle of the oven until golden, 8 to 10 minutes. Cool crisps completely on the baking sheet and remove carefully with a metal spatula. Crisps may be made up to 2 days in advance and kept between layers of wax paper in an airtight container at room temperature.
4. Makes about 9 crisps.
